hero.title: 11 Days, In the Buddha's Footsteps.
hero.titleAccent: Bodhgaya, Sarnath, Kushinagar and the Sacred Sites.
hero.intro: Walk in the footsteps of the Buddha. The great pilgrimage sites where he was enlightened, gave his first sermon and attained final nirvana, across the plains of Bihar and Uttar Pradesh, with Lumbini just over the border. Eleven days on the Buddhist trail.
hero.heroImageAlt: Buddhist pilgrimage Bodhgaya Mahabodhi temple India

overview.heading: 11 Days Buddhist Pilgrimage: The Full Picture
body: This 11 day pilgrimage follows the great sites of the Buddha's life across northern India and the Nepal border. From Bodhgaya, where he attained enlightenment beneath the Bodhi tree, to Sarnath, where he gave his first sermon, and Kushinagar, where he attained final nirvana, it traces the spiritual heart of Buddhism.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip is designed for Buddhist pilgrims and those drawn to the Buddha's teachings. A professional guide and a private vehicle link the sacred sites, including Rajgir and Nalanda, the great ancient university, with Lumbini, the Buddha's birthplace, just across the border in Nepal. It is a profound and well-paced journey.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Bodhgaya & the Mahabodhi Temple
items.body: The holiest site in Buddhism, where the Buddha attained enlightenment beneath the Bodhi tree. The Mahabodhi Temple complex is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a place of deep devotion.

items.title: Sarnath & Kushinagar
items.body: Sarnath, where the Buddha gave his first sermon, and Kushinagar, where he attained final nirvana, two of the four most sacred sites in Buddhism.

items.title: Nalanda, Rajgir & Lumbini
items.body: The ruins of the great Nalanda university, the hills of Rajgir, and Lumbini, the Buddha's birthplace just over the Nepal border, complete the pilgrimage.

days.title: Arrive Gaya / Bodhgaya
days.description: Met at Gaya airport and transferred to Bodhgaya, the holiest Buddhist site.

days.title: Bodhgaya Sightseeing
days.description: A full day at the sacred sites of Bodhgaya.

days.title: Bodhgaya to Rajgir & Nalanda
days.description: The hills of Rajgir and the ruins of Nalanda university.

days.title: To Patna / Vaishali
days.description: Travel via Vaishali, an important Buddhist site.

days.title: To Kushinagar
days.description: Drive to Kushinagar, where the Buddha attained final nirvana.

days.title: Kushinagar to Lumbini (Nepal)
days.description: Cross into Nepal to Lumbini, the Buddha's birthplace.

days.title: Lumbini to Sravasti
days.description: Return to India and drive to Sravasti.

days.title: Sravasti to Lucknow
days.description: Travel towards Lucknow with cultural stops.

days.title: Varanasi
days.description: Sunrise boat and the sacred city.

days.title: Depart Varanasi
days.description: Transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: The Buddhist Trail, Routed With Reverence
whyBook.body: The Buddhist sites are spread across Bihar, Uttar Pradesh and the Nepal border, with a crossing to manage. We handle the routing, the border and a guide versed in the Buddha's life. Dhesu has arranged pilgrimages since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Buddhist Pilgrimage Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 11 day pilgrimage follows the great sites of the Buddha's life across northern India and into Nepal. From Bodhgaya's enlightenment site to Sarnath, Kushinagar and Lumbini, it traces the spiritual heart of Buddhism.

With a professional guide and a private vehicle, the trip handles the considerable distances and the Nepal border crossing, with the ancient university of Nalanda and Rajgir along the way.

It is a profound journey for Buddhist pilgrims and anyone drawn to the Buddha's teachings.

sections.heading: The Four Great Sites and Beyond
sections.body:
Buddhism recognises four principal pilgrimage sites: Lumbini, where the Buddha was born; Bodhgaya, where he attained enlightenment; Sarnath, where he gave his first sermon; and Kushinagar, where he attained final nirvana. This pilgrimage covers all four.

It also includes Nalanda, the great ancient seat of Buddhist learning, Rajgir, Vaishali and Sravasti, giving a fuller picture of the Buddha's life and the spread of his teachings.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Gaya and Varanasi Airports (GAY / VNS). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Do I need a separate visa for the Nepal section?
items.answer: This pilgrimage crosses into Nepal to visit Lumbini. Visa requirements for Nepal differ from India, and Nepal offers visa on arrival for many nationalities. We will advise on the specific requirements for your passport before travel.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather across the plains. The cooler winter months are ideal for the many site visits. Summers are extremely hot, and the monsoon brings heavy rain that can affect travel.

items.question: Is this trip suitable for non-Buddhists?
items.answer: Yes. While it is a meaningful pilgrimage for Buddhists, the sites are also of great historical and cultural significance. Anyone interested in the life of the Buddha, ancient history or Indian heritage will find it rewarding.
